summary: Registers file suffix mappings for a known content type when suffix metadata exists.
signature_intent: Accepts a content type identifier and updates shared lookup mappings as a side effect.
behavior: Stops without changes when the content type has no associated suffixes.
Stores the content type's suffix list for reverse lookup.
Assigns each suffix to the content type when no better existing mapping is present.
Preserves an existing suffix mapping when it has higher configured priority.
On equal priority, preserves an existing application-class mapping over another competing mapping.
edge_cases: Missing or empty suffix metadata produces no result.
A generic binary mapping can be replaced by a more specific content type.
Existing mappings may remain unchanged when priority rules favor them.
forbidden_details: Variable names, repository names, source paths, and incidental loop mechanics are intentionally omitted.
